title: Molten Kenyan Malt Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Kenyan dish featuring molten sauce, wholegrain mustard, and nuka-pickled vegetables, perfect for a romantic date night
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cumin and coriander, and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced potatoes and cook for 3 minutes, until slightly golden brown.
Add the boneless chicken thighs and cook for 3 minutes, until browned on both sides.
Add the dried dates, nuka-vinegar, and wholegrain mustard, and stir to combine.
Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is thick and molten.
Stir in the chopped fresh cilantro and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
